name: Dolt
description: Git-like version controlled SQL database with MySQL compatibility

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted via CLI/Docker, Hosted Dolt managed service available

topology.comment: Supports primary-standby replication; multi-primary not fully supported

sharding.comment: Dolt is primarily a single-node database. It does not support native sharding. Data distribution follows Git-like clone/push/pull semantics.

replication-factor.comment: Primary-standby replication with configurable standby remotes

multi-region.comment: No native multi-region support; data can be cloned/pushed to multiple locations but not synchronized in real-time

cap-position.comment: Single-node with optional replication prioritizes consistency

consistency-model.comment: MySQL-compatible ACID semantics on single node

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions with MySQL-compatible semantics

transaction-scope.comment: Single-node database supports full table transactions

distributed-transactions.comment: Single-node database; no distributed transaction coordination

sql-compatibility.comment: Drop-in MySQL replacement with wire protocol compatibility

joins.comment: Full SQL joins on single node; no distributed joins as it's not a distributed database

max-cluster-size.comment: Dolt is designed as single-node; replication is primary-standby, not clustered

write-throughput.comment: 36.85 TPS on TPC-C benchmark vs MySQL's 93.54 TPS (2.54x multiplier)

read-latency-p99.comment: P99 figures not published; mean sysbench latency ~1.05x MySQL

online-schema-changes.comment: Supports online DDL via version control branches

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Git-like version control allows checkout of any historical commit

backup-strategy.comment: Native version control provides incremental snapshots via commits

kubernetes-operator.comment: No official Kubernetes operator; Docker images available

rust-driver.comment: Uses MySQL rust crate (mysql); tested by DoltHub

go-driver.comment: Official dolthub/driver for embedded use; also supports go-sql-driver/mysql

dotnet-driver.comment: Uses MySQL .NET connectors; tested with EF Core

python-driver.comment: Uses mysql.connector, pymysql; tested by DoltHub

ruby-driver.comment: Uses MySQL Ruby drivers; Rails integration documented

orm-support.comment: Django, SQLAlchemy, Prisma, GORM, Hibernate, EF Core, Rails all tested

free-tier.comment: DoltHub hosts public databases for free; Hosted Dolt has paid tiers starting ~$50/month

pricing-model.comment: Hosted Dolt charges based on instance size and storage

cost-estimate-small.comment: Starting price for Hosted Dolt; single-node typically sufficient

change-data-capture.comment: No built-in CDC; supports Debezium via MySQL binlog replication

event-streaming.comment: Via Debezium connector using MySQL binlog; not native integration

stackoverflow-tags.comment: No significant Stack Overflow tag presence found
